title: Chamisme ciliolata
taxonomy: Chamisme ciliolata is a member of the flowering plant family Ericaceae. It is a species of shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. The classification of Chamisme ciliolata includes its kingdom as Plantae, its phylum as Magnoliophyta, and its order as Ericales. At the family level, it belongs to the Ericaceae family, and at the genus and species level, it is categorized as Chamisme ciliolata. Further classification at the rank of genus is uncertain, but it is widely accepted as Chamisme.
uses: Chamisme ciliolata, a plant native to the mountainous regions of the Americas, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its diverse health benefits. The plant's extracts have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating various infections. In some cultures, Chamisme ciliolata is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its purported ability to regulate gut health. Additionally, the plant has been studied for its potential anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Further investigation is necessary to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential applications of Chamisme ciliolata.
